tools.name: search_by_regex
tools.description: Fast text-based search that finds exact pattern matches within files or directories, utilizing the ripgrep command for efficient searching.

tools.name: view_files
tools.description: View up to 3 files simultaneously in batch mode for faster information gathering.

tools.name: list_dir
tools.description: You can use this tool to view files of the specified directory.

tools.name: write_to_file
tools.description: You can use this tool to write content to a file with precise control over creation/rewrite behavior.

tools.name: update_file

tools.name: edit_file_fast_apply

tools.name: rename_file
tools.description: You can use this tool to move or rename an existing file.

tools.name: delete_file
tools.description: You can use this tool to delete files, you can delete multi files in one toolcall, and you MUST make sure the files is exist before deleting.

tools.name: run_command
tools.description: You can use this tool to PROPOSE a command to run on behalf of the user.

tools.name: check_command_status
tools.description: You can use this tool to get the status of a previously executed command by its Command ID ( non-blocking command ).

tools.name: stop_command
tools.description: This tool allows you to terminate a currently running command( the command MUST be previously executed command. ).

tools.name: open_preview
tools.description: You can use this tool to show the available preview URL to user if you have started a local server successfully in a previous toolcall, which user can open it in the browser.

tools.name: web_search
tools.description: This tool can be used to search the internet, which should be used with caution, as frequent searches result in a bad user experience and excessive costs.

tools.name: finish
tools.description: The final tool of this session, when you think you have archived the goal of user requirement, you should use this tool to mark it as finish.
